title: The Riverside Murder
summary: Clara Finch, assistant to Mayor Hart, was found dead on the riverbank at dawn. The town is whispering. Three suspects have shaky alibis.
location_hint: Start at the Detective Agency, then visit the river crime scene east of downtown.

evidence.name: Broken Pocket Watch
evidence.description: Found clutched in Clara's hand. Stopped at 11:42 PM. Engraved 'M.R.' on the back.

evidence.name: Boot Print Cast
evidence.description: Size 11 work boot, deep tread, right heel worn inward. Matches warehouse boots.

evidence.name: Torn Note
evidence.description: Half a note: 'Stop looking into the books or—' ink smudged by river water.

evidence.name: Torn Ledger Page
evidence.description: Town warehouse accounts. Several payments marked 'consulting' to M.R. — totals don't add up.

evidence.name: Office Spare Key
evidence.description: Vera admits she had a spare key to Clara's office. Doesn't prove violence.

evidence.name: IOU Slip
evidence.description: Owen owed Clara money for a failed side job. Motive, but his boot size is 9.

suspects.name: Vera Lang
suspects.bio: Ambitious, sharp-tongued. Wanted Clara's job. Was seen arguing with her last week.

suspects.name: Marcus Reed
suspects.bio: Quiet, strong, always in work boots. Initials M.R. Runs night inventory alone.

suspects.name: Owen Pike
suspects.bio: Charming liar. Owes half the town money. Size 9 boots, bright sneakers.

deputy_cole.speaker: Deputy Cole
deputy_cole.text: Detective. Clara Finch. Riverbank. No signs of a struggle by the waterline — she was moved, or she fell after the blow.

deputy_cole.speaker: Deputy Cole
deputy_cole.text: Last seen leaving the town office around 10:30 PM. Body found at 5:40 AM by Ben who jogs the river path. Watch stopped at 11:42.

deputy_cole.speaker: Deputy Cole
deputy_cole.text: Ben Carter. Solid kid. Shaken up. He's still at the diner if you want a statement.

deputy_cole.speaker: Deputy Cole
deputy_cole.text: Three names keep coming up: Vera Lang at the clerk's desk, Marcus Reed at the warehouse, and Owen Pike who floats between jobs. Don't accuse without proof — Hollow doesn't forget.

deputy_cole.speaker: Deputy Cole
deputy_cole.text: Radio if you need backup. And Detective… Clara was good people.

witness_ben.speaker: Ben Carter
witness_ben.text: I was jogging. Saw something pale near the reeds. Thought it was trash. It wasn't. I called the deputy right away.

witness_ben.speaker: Ben Carter
witness_ben.text: Not clearly. But earlier, around midnight, I heard a truck near the warehouse road. Heavy diesel. Didn't think much of it until… this.

witness_ben.speaker: Ben Carter
witness_ben.text: Toward the warehouse lot. Marcus works nights sometimes. Or used to.

witness_ben.speaker: Ben Carter
witness_ben.text: Didn't see headlights on the river path. Just that truck sound inland.

witness_ben.speaker: Ben Carter
witness_ben.text: I keep replaying it. If I can help again, I'm at the diner most mornings.

suspect_vera.speaker: Vera Lang
suspect_vera.text: I know what people say. That I wanted her desk. Ambition isn't murder, Detective.

options.text: You had a key to her office.

suspect_vera.speaker: Vera Lang
suspect_vera.text: Home. Alone. Reading. No, I don't have a witness. Not everyone lives their life for an audience.

suspect_vera.speaker: Vera Lang
suspect_vera.text: Yes, I had a spare. Mayor asked me to lock up when Clara left early. I returned it to the board this morning — after I heard.

suspect_vera.speaker: Vera Lang
suspect_vera.text: Warehouse staff still pick up sealed envelopes from her outbox. Marcus Reed came by often. Too often.

suspect_vera.speaker: Vera Lang
suspect_vera.text: Those 'consulting' lines? Clara flagged them last month. She was digging into warehouse payouts. Someone didn't like that.

suspect_vera.speaker: Vera Lang
suspect_vera.text: Marcus signs the night sheets. If the numbers are fiction, his name is on the fiction.

suspect_vera.speaker: Vera Lang
suspect_vera.text: Find who did this. Clara and I weren't friends — but she didn't deserve the river.

suspect_marcus.speaker: Marcus Reed
suspect_marcus.text: I already talked to Cole. I was at the warehouse doing inventory. Alone. That's my job.
suspect_marcus.lie_tell: He looks at his boots instead of your eyes when he says 'alone'.

options.text: Your watch was in her hand.

suspect_marcus.speaker: Marcus Reed
suspect_marcus.text: Plenty of people have initials. Maybe she bought it used. Maybe you're stretching.
suspect_marcus.lie_tell: His jaw tightens. Sweat at the temple.

options.text: It stopped at 11:42 — when Ben heard your truck.

suspect_marcus.speaker: Marcus Reed
suspect_marcus.text: I work the river road. Mud is not a confession.

suspect_marcus.speaker: Marcus Reed
suspect_marcus.text: Consulting fees. Contractors. Not my department to question the mayor's envelopes.
suspect_marcus.lie_tell: His voice jumps half a note on 'not my department'.

options.text: Clara was about to expose you.

suspect_marcus.speaker: Marcus Reed
suspect_marcus.text: She wouldn't drop it. Said she'd go to the papers. I only meant to scare her at the river. She slipped. Or she didn't. Either way it's done.

suspect_marcus.speaker: Marcus Reed
suspect_marcus.text: You've got your story. See if a jury likes it.

suspect_owen.speaker: Owen Pike
suspect_owen.text: Detective! Always a pleasure. I didn't kill Clara. I liked Clara. She loaned me money, which is more than most do.

suspect_owen.speaker: Owen Pike
suspect_owen.text: Forty bucks and a busted porch job. Here's the IOU — I'm not proud, but I'm not a killer.

suspect_owen.speaker: Owen Pike
suspect_owen.text: Bartender Sam will tell you I closed the bar stool until 1 AM. Ask him.

suspect_owen.speaker: Owen Pike
suspect_owen.text: Size 9, baby. And I wore sneakers that night — white ones. Mud would ruin the look.

options.text: You're free to go… for now.

suspect_owen.speaker: Owen Pike
suspect_owen.text: If you clear me, maybe buy me a coffee. Hollow's going to need cheering up.

bartender_sam.speaker: Sam
bartender_sam.text: What can I pour you, Detective? Coffee's always free for the badge.

bartender_sam.speaker: Sam
bartender_sam.text: Stuck to that stool like gum. From ten to one. Loud about a card game he lost. Didn't leave.

bartender_sam.speaker: Sam
bartender_sam.text: On the house. You look like you haven't slept since the river news.

mayor_hart.speaker: Mayor Hart
mayor_hart.text: Clara kept this town running. Find who did this. Quietly, if you can — but thoroughly.

options.text: I'll need access to her files.

mayor_hart.speaker: Mayor Hart
mayor_hart.text: Vera can open the office. Clara was reviewing warehouse contracts. She left a note on my desk: 'Something's wrong with M.R. payments.'

mayor_hart.speaker: Mayor Hart
mayor_hart.text: Not enemies — friction. Vera wanted more responsibility. Marcus hated paperwork. Owen hated paying people back.

mayor_hart.speaker: Mayor Hart
mayor_hart.text: Mystery Hollow is watching, Detective. So am I.

correct.text: Marcus Reed breaks in interrogation. Clara discovered he was skimming warehouse 'consulting' funds. He confronted her by the river; the argument turned fatal. His watch, boots, the note, and the ledger seal the case. Mystery Hollow exhales — justice, hard-earned.

wrong_suspect_vera.text: You accuse Vera Lang. The town turns on her. Weeks later, the real trail — Marcus and the warehouse books — surfaces without you. Your reputation takes a hit. Vera never quite forgives you.

wrong_suspect_owen.text: Owen Pike is loud, broke, and easy to blame. A jury wouldn't. The real killer walks free long enough to burn the remaining ledgers. Hollow learns to doubt your badge.

wrong_suspect_marcus.text: You name Marcus but without enough paper trail. He walks on reasonable doubt. The case goes cold — for now.

wrong_generic.text: The accusation doesn't stick. Evidence was incomplete. The case cools, and so does the town's faith in you.
